fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the fixed asset.

fields.name: Search Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a search description for the fixed asset.

fields.name: Description 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional description for the fixed asset. You can enter a maximum of 30 characters, both numbers and letters.

fields.name: FA Class Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the class that the fixed asset belongs to.

fields.name: FA Subclass Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the subclass of the class that the fixed asset belongs to.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a dimension value code for a dimension. To see the dimension value codes you have set up for this dimension in the IC Dimension Values window, click the field.
If you enter a dimension value code in this field, the program will use the corresponding dimension value as a default each time you enter the account on an entry line. Default dimension values are only suggestions and you can change them on the entry line, for example in a journal.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a dimension value code for a dimension. To see the dimension value codes you have set up for this dimension in the IC Dimension Values window, click the field.
If you enter a dimension value code in this field, the program will use the corresponding dimension value as a default each time you enter the account on an entry line. Default dimension values are only suggestions and you can change them on the entry line, for example in a journal.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the location of the fixed asset. Location codes can be used to identify different warehouses, for example. You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters. You can also use the FA Location Code field, designed specifically for fixed assets, to register the location of the fixed asset.
To see the location codes in the Location table, click the field.

fields.name: FA Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the location, such as a building, where the fixed asset is located.

fields.name: Vendor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the vendor from which you purchased this fixed asset.

fields.name: Main Asset/Component
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the fixed asset is a main fixed asset or a component of a fixed asset.

fields.name: Component of Main Asset
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the main fixed asset.

fields.name: Budgeted Asset
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the asset is for budgeting purposes.

fields.name: Warranty Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the warranty expiration date of the fixed asset.

fields.name: Responsible Employee
fields.tooltip: Specifies which employee is responsible for the fixed asset.

fields.name: Serial No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the fixed asset's serial number.

fields.name: Last Date Modified
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the fixed asset card was last modified.

fields.name: Insured
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the fixed asset is linked to an insurance policy.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether any comments have been entered for this fixed asset.
To view a comment or create a new one, click Related Information, point to Fixed Asset, and then click Comment. A comment sheet window will appear, in which you can see the existing comments and create new ones. See also the Comment Line window.
You can also enter a comment in the Notes FactBox.

fields.name: Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the related record is blocked from being posted in transactions, for example a customer that is declared insolvent or an item that is placed in quarantine.

fields.name: Maintenance Vendor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the vendor who performs repairs and maintenance on the fixed asset.

fields.name: Under Maintenance
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the fixed asset is currently being repaired.

fields.name: Next Service Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the next scheduled service date for the fixed asset. This is used as a filter in the Maintenance - Next Service report.

fields.name: Inactive
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the fixed asset is inactive (for example, if the asset is not in service or is obsolete).

fields.name: FA Posting Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Defines a filter to see entries for a certain fixed asset posting date.
If this field contains the starting and ending dates for a time period, various reports and statistics windows will display amounts for the fixed asset in that time period only.
You can enter a date or a time interval. There are special rules for how they should be entered:

Meaning
Sample Expression
Entries Included

Equal to

12 15 00

Only those posted on 12 15 00.


Interval

12 15 00..01 15 01
..12 15 00

Those posted on dates between and including 12 15 00 and 01 15 01.
Those posted on 12 15 00 or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 00|12 16 00

Those posted on either 12 15 00 or 12 16 00. If there are entries posted on both days, they will all be displayed.


You can also combine the various format types:

Sample Expression
Entries Included

12 15 00|12 01 00..12 10 00

Entries posted either on 12 15 00 or on dates between and including 12 01 00 and 12 10 00.


..12 14 00|12 30 00..

Entries posted on 12 14 00 or earlier, or entries posted on 12 30 00 or later - that is, all entries except those posted on dates between and including 12 15 00 and 12 29 00.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Contains the code for the number series that was used to assign the fixed asset's number.

fields.name: FA Posting Group
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Acquired
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the fixed asset has been acquired.

fields.name: Image
fields.tooltip: Specifies the picture that has been inserted for the fixed asset.
